chapter_title.ar: باب الْخُشُوعِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ وَ كَرَاهِيَةِ الْعَبَثِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – The humbleness during the Salāt and abhorrence of the frivolities

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/77)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/77)
text: كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( صلوات الله عليه ) إِذَا قَامَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ تَغَيَّرَ لَوْنُهُ فَإِذَا سَجَدَ لَمْ يَرْفَعْ رَأْسَهُ حَتَّى يَرْفَضَّ عَرَقاً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin ShAzān, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Rabie Bin Abdullah, from Al Fuzayl Bin Yasaar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘It was that whenever Aliasws Bin Al-Husaynasws, stood regarding the Salāt, hisasws colour changed. So when heasws performed Sajdah, did not raise hisasws head until heasws perspired profusely’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from Hammad ibn Isa from Rib‘i ibn ‘Abd Allah from al-Fudayl ibn Yasar who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that when Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would stand up for Salah (prayer), the color of his face change and in sajdah he remained so long until (his face) became drenched with perspiration (and tears).”
